meeting: Post Quantum Transaction Signature (PQTS) Breakout #7 - January 2025

ephemeral_keys_implementation.highlight: Riva Labs demo: NiceTry wallet supports ECDSA and WOTS modes with rotation
WOTS mode: 93K gas validation, 460-byte signatures, quantum-safe by design
Live demo: DeFi interaction (Lido staking) working on testnet with key rotation
WOTS backup signers address transaction failure scenarios (wrong nonce, gas limits)

sphinx_variants.highlight: NIST published SPHINCS- variant (2^24 signatures) two weeks ago for firmware signing
SPHINCS- SHA-2 variant: 142K gas pure verify, FIPS-compliant path for institutions
Hardware constraint: SPHINCS- requires HSM (1-2 min signing), not hardware wallet compatible

jardin_design.highlight: JARDIN: Hybrid stateless/stateful design combining SPHINCS- with FORS few-time signatures
Type 1 (stateless): 400K gas, 6KB signature; Type 2 (stateful): 170K gas, 128 transactions per lane
Ledger implementation working: 3-second compact signing, 45-second stateful signing
FORS prevents catastrophic key reuse from fault injection/state rollback attacks

zk_proof_approach.highlight: Longfow ZK proving: 87ms client-side, hides public key via zero-knowledge ECDSA proof
Current implementation: ~2M gas, 20KB proofs; optimizations in progress (column reduction, designated verifier)
EIP-7702 delegation protects assets post-key-exposure by requiring ZK proof for withdrawals

action_items.action: Publish ETH Research posts for SPHINCS- and JARDIN designs
Provide feedback on Mahdi's ZK proof approach via ETH Research thread

targets.target: Next PQTS breakout call in two weeks
